title: Expense ratio — check what stuck
intro: Four quick checks on how fees really work.

questions.prompt: The Total Expense Ratio is charged…
choices: On your entire balance, every year
Only if the fund beats its benchmark
questions.explanation: TER is skimmed daily from NAV on the whole balance, every year — regardless of whether the fund wins or loses.

questions.prompt: A Direct plan vs the Regular plan of the same fund holds…
choices: The same portfolio, minus distributor commission
questions.explanation: Direct and Regular are the identical portfolio; Direct just strips the distributor trail, so its TER is lower and NAV grows faster.

questions.prompt: Why does a 1% higher TER cost far more than 1% of your money over 20 years?
choices: Because it lowers your growth rate, so the shortfall compounds
questions.explanation: A fee reduces the compounding rate, so the gap grows geometrically year on year — the 'lost to fees' curve bends upward.

questions.prompt: Best way to compare two funds' expense ratios?
choices: Always pick the lowest TER anywhere
Compare within the same category and job
Compare a Direct plan to a Regular plan
questions.explanation: An index fund and an active mid-cap do different jobs — compare TER within a category, and weigh it as one input, not the whole verdict.
